examples.title: Algorithm: Dynamic Memory Management Process
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the complete process of allocating, using, resizing, and freeing dynamic memory
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DynamicMemoryManagement
INPUT: number of elements needed
OUTPUT: pointer to allocated memory block

BEGIN
 // Step 1: Request memory allocation
 FUNCTION AllocateMemory(size)
 pointer ← malloc(size)
 IF pointer = NULL THEN
 PRINT "Allocation failed"
 EXIT PROGRAM
 END IF
 RETURN pointer
 END FUNCTION

 // Step 2: Allocate using calloc (for arrays)
 FUNCTION AllocateArray(numElements, elementSize)
 pointer ← calloc(numElements, elementSize)
 IF pointer = NULL THEN
 PRINT "Allocation failed"
 EXIT PROGRAM
 END IF
 RETURN pointer
 END FUNCTION

 // Step 3: Resize existing memory block
 FUNCTION ResizeMemory(oldPointer, newSize)
 newPointer ← realloc(oldPointer, newSize)
 IF newPointer = NULL THEN
 PRINT "Reallocation failed"
 FREE oldPointer // Don't lose original memory
 EXIT PROGRAM
 END IF
 RETURN newPointer
 END FUNCTION

 // Step 4: Free/deallocate memory
 FUNCTION FreeMemory(pointer)
 IF pointer ≠ NULL THEN
 FREE pointer
 pointer ← NULL // Prevent dangling pointer
 END IF
 END FUNCTION

 // Main program demonstrating usage
 BEGIN MAIN
 // Allocate memory for 5 integers
 ptr ← AllocateMemory(5 × sizeof(int))
 
 // Use the allocated memory
 FOR i FROM 0 TO 4 DO
 ptr[i] ← i × 10
 END FOR
 
 // Resize to 10 integers
 ptr ← ResizeMemory(ptr, 10 × sizeof(int))
 
 // Use expanded memory
 FOR i FROM 5 TO 9 DO
 ptr[i] ← i × 10
 END FOR
 
 // Free memory when done
 FreeMemory(ptr)
 
 PRINT "Memory management completed successfully"
 END MAIN
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the four essential dynamic memory functions: malloc for initial allocation, calloc for zero-initialized array allocation, realloc for resizing existing blocks, and free for deallocation. The algorithm emphasizes proper error checking after each allocation and setting pointers to NULL after freeing to prevent dangling pointers.

examples.title: C: Complete Dynamic Memory Allocation Implementation
examples.description: Full C program demonstrating malloc, calloc, realloc, and free with proper error handling
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates all four dynamic memory allocation functions. Key points: malloc allocates uninitialized memory, calloc allocates zero-initialized memory, realloc resizes existing blocks while preserving data, and free deallocates memory. The program shows proper NULL checks after each allocation, setting pointers to NULL after free() to prevent dangling pointer bugs, and the difference between initialized and uninitialized memory.
examples.timeComplexity: malloc: O(1) average, calloc: O(n) due to zero-initialization, realloc: O(n) worst case when data must be copied, free: O(1)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of elements allocated

examples.title: C: Dynamic Memory in Data Structures - Linked List
examples.description: Implementation of a linked list demonstrating dynamic memory allocation for data structures
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates how dynamic memory allocation is essential for implementing linked lists. Each node is created using malloc() and memory is properly freed using free() when nodes are deleted or when the list is no longer needed. The example shows proper memory management practices: checking malloc return values, setting pointers to NULL after free() to prevent dangling pointers, and ensuring every malloc has a corresponding free() to avoid memory leaks.
examples.timeComplexity: CreateNode: O(1), InsertAtBeginning: O(1), InsertAtEnd: O(n), DeleteNode: O(n), FreeList: O(n)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of nodes in the linked list
